titel: The Swedish National Audit Office's report on the state's work with Agenda 2030.
html:
In this document, the government presents its assessment of the observations and recommendations made by the Swedish National Audit Office (Riksrevisionen) in the report "The State's Work with Agenda 2030" (RiR 2025:22).

The Swedish National Audit Office's overall conclusion is that the state's work with Agenda 2030 has not been effective. The audit cannot demonstrate that a more effective approach to implementing Agenda 2030 would have contributed to even higher achievement of the goals, but the Swedish National Audit Office assesses that the conditions for achieving higher goals would have been better.

The government welcomes the Swedish National Audit Office's review of the state's work with Agenda 2030, but has a somewhat different assessment regarding the observations made by the Swedish National Audit Office. The government intends to clarify its division of responsibilities for policy coherence.

With this document, the government considers the Swedish National Audit Office's report to be finalized.
tldr: The Swedish National Audit Office has reviewed how the Swedish government is working towards Agenda 2030, which are the United Nations' global goals for sustainable development. They believe that the government's efforts have not been sufficient to achieve these goals.
lattlast:
The Swedish National Audit Office is an agency that monitors how the state uses money and fulfills its duties. In a new report, they state that the state's work on Agenda 2030 has not been effective. Agenda 2030 is a plan with many goals to create a better and more sustainable world.

The National Audit Office believes that the state could have achieved better results if it had worked in a different way. They believe that the chances of achieving the goals would have been greater if the work had been more well-planned.

The government has now read the report and responded to it. The government does not entirely agree with the National Audit Office in all parts of the report. However, they want to clarify who is responsible for steering policy in a consistent direction.

With this response, the government states that they have finished reviewing the report and that the matter is now closed. They have received the information and made their assessment of what needs to be done going forward.
